sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds: Battle Born Pond, Davis Creek Pond, Idlewild Ponds, Marilyn's Pond, Paradise Ponds, Rancho San Rafael Pond, Sparks Marina Pond, Verdi Pond, Virginia Lake and Wilson Common. Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours parks are open to public use.

sources.title: Idlewild Pond — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Repairs on the ponds have been completed, and trout have been stocked back into the small pond. Power bait and power worms off the bottom or under a bobber are both effective methods on the little pond. Small spinners and kastmasters can be good in the deeper water. (Stocking record: 999 Rainbow Trout, 10.0 inches, stocked 06-12-2026.)

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Idlewild Ponds are open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit is stated for Idlewild Ponds in NDOW's current regulation table; the limit applies to the combined daily/possession total across all game fish species.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds (incl. Idlewild Ponds)
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (all species combined) at Idlewild Ponds.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish.

parameters.note: NDOW's commission-regulation table lists hours for Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds generically as 'hours parks are open to public use'; no fixed clock times or sunrise/sunset offsets are specified for Idlewild Ponds (within Idlewild Park, a City of Reno park).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing at Idlewild Ponds is allowed during the hours the park is open to the public.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours parks are open to public use

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older; no water-specific permit exists for Idlewild Ponds.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older at Idlewild Ponds; no license is required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
